purpose: Provides real-time observable regime classification for rates vol without the identification lag of formal Markov-switching models.
components: MOVE < 80: Low rates vol regime — expectations-driven yield curve, reliable recession signals, carry strategies favorable
MOVE 80-100: Transitional — elevated uncertainty but not crisis, monitor for direction
MOVE 100-140 (current): High-floor regime — term premium contaminates curve signals, credit conditions tightening at margin, 18+ month persistence
MOVE > 140: Crisis regime — active deleveraging, curve signals dominated by risk premia
current_assessment: MOVE at 100-120 in the high-floor regime for 18+ months, longest since 2008-2012. Four structural drivers (BoJ normalization, Fed terminal rate ambiguity, $2T+ issuance, basis trade fragility) support continued elevation. Question is whether this is the new normal requiring model re-baselining.
limitations: Thresholds are approximate and may need recalibration in structurally higher nominal growth world
Does not capture within-regime dynamics changed by microstructure evolution
Single-dimensional — does not incorporate cross-asset information
Cannot distinguish between different macro causes of elevated MOVE
